generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System component for occupancy detection, modeled within BIM environment and integrated into the DT semantic foundation. Spatial distribution aligned with BIM geometric models."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System component for temperature measurement, modeled within BIM environment and integrated into the DT semantic foundation. Spatial distribution aligned with BIM geometric models."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System component for energy consumption monitoring, modeled within BIM environment and integrated into the DT semantic foundation. Spatial distribution aligned with BIM geometric models."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

instance StructuralHealthSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System component for structural health monitoring, modeled within BIM environment and integrated into the DT semantic foundation. Spatial distribution aligned with BIM geometric models."
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralHealthDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System component for environmental condition monitoring (e.g., humidity, air quality), modeled within BIM environment and integrated into the DT semantic foundation. Spatial distribution aligned with BIM geometric models."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

instance StructuralHealthDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralHealthSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalConditionSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BimGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantically rich 3D representation of the building providing geometric, spatial, and functional data as the foundation for DT artifacts. Inherited from BIM and enriched with runtime data."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimIntegrationEnabler, UnrealEngineEnabler
]

instance BuildingControlSystemModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Structured representation of Building Control System components (sensors, actuators, controllers) extracted via Model-Driven Engineering principles. Ensures interoperability between physical and digital domains."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimIntegrationEnabler, EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance ThermalDynamicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulation model for thermal dynamics within the building, enabling what-if analysis and energy consumption optimization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngineEnabler
]

instance StructuralIntegrityModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulation model for structural integrity assessment, enabling predictive maintenance and lifecycle management."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngineEnabler
]

instance EnergyConsumptionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time and historical energy consumption metrics collected from sensors and integrated into the DT for analytics and optimization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnergyAnalyticsEnabler, OptimizationService
]

instance OccupancyData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time occupancy data collected from sensors, used for space utilization analysis and operational efficiency metrics."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo OccupancyAnalyticsEnabler
]

instance EnvironmentalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time environmental data (temperature, humidity, air quality) collected from sensors, used for indoor climate optimization and health monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted, EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnvironmentalAnalyticsEnabler, OptimizationService
]

instance StructuralHealthData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time structural health monitoring data collected from sensors, used for predictive maintenance and integrity assessment."
    DTDFVocab:fromData StructuralHealthDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StructuralHealthAnalyticsEnabler, PredictiveMaintenanceService
]

instance HistoricalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical data including sensor logs, maintenance records, and environmental data stored for long-term analytics and machine learning training."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MachineLearningEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BimIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering framework for integrating BIM models with DT artifacts, automating generation of deployable simulation models and runtime configurations."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService, VisualizationService
]

instance EclipseHonoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time data exchange protocol facilitating secure, scalable, and interoperable communication between IoT devices, sensors, and the digital twin core. Enables event-driven data processing."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, DataSynchronizationService
]

instance EclipseDittoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital twin state management system enabling bidirectional data exchange and real-time synchronization between physical and digital components."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, DataSynchronizationService, OptimizationService
]

instance UnrealEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "High-fidelity visualization platform supporting real-time rendering of the digital twin with interactive 3D environments for immersive user interaction."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService, DecisionSupportService
]

instance SimulationEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Physics-based and data-driven simulation engines for modeling thermal dynamics, structural integrity, and system interactions. Enables what-if analysis and optimization."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService, OptimizationService, DecisionSupportService
]

instance EnergyAnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Domain-specific analytics framework for energy consumption data, enabling cost modeling, operational efficiency metrics, and lifecycle management recommendations."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService, OptimizationService
]

instance OccupancyAnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Analytics framework for occupancy data, enabling space utilization analysis, operational efficiency metrics, and lifecycle management recommendations."
    DTDFVocab:enables DecisionSupportService, OptimizationService
]

instance EnvironmentalAnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Analytics framework for environmental data (temperature, humidity, air quality), enabling indoor climate optimization and health monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationService, DecisionSupportService
]

instance StructuralHealthAnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Analytics framework for structural health data, enabling predictive maintenance and integrity assessment."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveMaintenanceService, DecisionSupportService
]

instance MachineLearningEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Machine learning framework for training models on historical and real-time data, enabling advanced predictive analytics and automation."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService, OptimizationService
]

instance IntegrationFrameworkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware solution (message brokers, API gateways) ensuring seamless interoperability between heterogeneous data sources (BMS, IoT, legacy systems)."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataSynchronizationService, RealTimeMonitoringService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Aggregates and visualizes live data from IoT sensors, environmental systems, and structural health monitors to track asset performance (e.g., energy consumption, occupancy, equipment status)."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimePerformanceInsight, EquipmentStatusAction
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Leverages historical and real-time data to forecast system failures, maintenance needs, or operational inefficiencies (e.g., predictive maintenance for HVAC or structural degradation)."
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveMaintenanceInsight, FailureForecastInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Applies algorithmic models (e.g., machine learning or simulation-based optimization) to refine system configurations for energy efficiency, cost reduction, or lifecycle adaptability (e.g., dynamic lighting/ventilation control)."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyEfficiencyInsight, CostReductionInsight, AdaptiveConfigurationAction
]

instance DecisionSupportService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables stakeholders (e.g., architects, facility managers, operators) with data-driven insights for design adjustments, refurbishment planning, or operational adaptations during the asset's lifecycle."
    DTDFVocab:provides DesignAdjustmentInsight, RefurbishmentRecommendationInsight, OperationalAdaptationAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offers immersive, multi-dimensional representations (e.g., 3D models, dashboards, or augmented reality overlays) to contextualize data for collaborative analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides ImmersiveVisualizationInsight, CollaborativeAnalysisAction
]

instance DataSynchronizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Ensures bidirectional consistency between physical building and its digital twin through real-time synchronization mechanisms, including event-driven updates and preprocessing steps."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataConsistencyInsight, SynchronizationAction
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Specialized service for forecasting equipment failures and recommending maintenance actions based on structural health and operational data."
    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenanceRecommendationInsight, MaintenanceScheduleAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimePerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Aggregated real-time metrics on asset performance including energy consumption, occupancy, and equipment status for immediate operational awareness."
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Forecasted maintenance needs based on predictive analytics of sensor data, including failure probabilities and recommended intervention timelines."
]

instance FailureForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted system failures or operational inefficiencies derived from historical and real-time data analysis."
]

instance EnergyEfficiencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized energy consumption metrics and recommendations for reducing operational costs through dynamic system configurations."
]

instance CostReductionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Financial impact analysis of operational adjustments, including lifecycle cost savings from predictive maintenance and energy optimization."
]

instance AdaptiveConfigurationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustments to system configurations (e.g., lighting, ventilation) based on real-time data and optimization algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DesignAdjustmentInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Data-driven recommendations for design modifications to improve operational efficiency or address identified inefficiencies."
]

instance RefurbishmentRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Strategic recommendations for building refurbishment based on lifecycle performance data and predictive analytics."
]

instance OperationalAdaptationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Manual or automated operational adjustments (e.g., equipment recalibration, workflow changes) based on decision support insights."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ImmersiveVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D representations and dashboards providing contextualized data visualization for collaborative decision-making."
]

instance CollaborativeAnalysisAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Facilitated multi-stakeholder analysis sessions using immersive visualization tools to align on operational strategies."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DataConsistencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Monitored bidirectional data consistency between physical and digital domains, ensuring synchronization accuracy."
]

instance SynchronizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated data synchronization processes between physical sensors and digital twin representations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance MaintenanceRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detailed maintenance recommendations including priority levels, required resources, and estimated completion times."
]

instance MaintenanceScheduleAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated or manual scheduling of maintenance activities based on predictive insights and operational constraints."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EquipmentStatusAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated alerts and status updates for equipment based on real-time monitoring data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control flows from the Digital Twin (DT) to physical infrastructure are managed through bidirectional communication, enabling Facility Managers to operate and monitor physical systems via the DT interface. The framework supports automated commissioning and real-time adjustments, with commands routed through middleware (e.g., Eclipse Hono and Ditto) to ensure seamless execution. While the text confirms the existence of control mechanisms, specific details such as command types (e.g., HVAC adjustments, lighting controls), message formats, or validation/authentication protocols (e.g., digital signatures, role-based access) are not provided. Feedback systems are implied through bidirectional data exchange, but conflict resolution mechanisms (e.g., priority-based arbitration) and command execution confirmation (e.g., acknowledgment tokens) are not explicitly documented. The system emphasizes adaptability via runtime configuration, though security and reliability safeguards remain abstract."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure **continuous synchronization** between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, enabling instantaneous bidirectional updates. The framework supports **dynamic operational needs** by aligning temporal granularity with the AECO (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations) domain, though specific synchronization frequencies (e.g., millisecond, hourly) or latency requirements are not explicitly defined. The system prioritizes **low-latency interactions** for real-time monitoring and control, as implied by the role of Facility Managers interacting with deployed Digital Twins for operational oversight. Historical or multi-scale temporal alignment (e.g., retrospective analysis) is not addressed in the provided context, focusing instead on **live operational synchronization** as the primary temporal characteristic."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a **coordinated constellation architecture** centered on the **DT Framework**, which acts as a **central hub** for orchestration, bidirectional data exchange, and **holistic life-cycle management**. The architecture is **modular and service-oriented**, with **standardized extension points** enabling **plug-and-play integration** of custom services (e.g., analytics, IoT gateways, or third-party APIs). Key orchestration mechanisms include:
  - **Role-based coordination**: The system defines distinct roles (**Building Control Engineers**, **Facility Managers**, **DT Engineers**) with **scope-specific responsibilities**, ensuring modular contributions to the DT life cycle (e.g., deployment, monitoring, extension).
  - **Runtime configuration management**: Enables **dynamic reconfiguration** of the constellation without full redeployment, allowing services to adapt to operational changes (e.g., adding a new sensor network or updating control algorithms).
  - **Bidirectional data flow**: The Framework ensures **synchronized data exchange** between physical assets and digital models, as well as across **heterogeneous DT instances** (e.g., linking a structural twin to an energy twin).
  - **Resource management**: Implicitly addressed through **scalability requirements** (from individual components to entire infrastructure systems) and **maintainability** via low-overhead updates, suggesting **elastic resource allocation** (e.g., cloud-based scaling for peak loads).
  The constellation leverages **enriched BIM models** as the foundational data layer, transforming static representations into **operational DTs** through **coordinated service integration**. The architecture prioritizes **interoperability** (via standardized extension points) and **accessibility** for users with varying technical expertise, ensuring a **unified yet extensible** system."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the AECO (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations) domain spans multiple phases, including **conceptualization, design, construction, operation, and decommissioning**, with a focus on **real-time synchronization** between physical assets and their digital counterparts. Representation types vary by phase: **BIM (Building Information Modeling)** is critical for design and construction, while **sensor-driven data models** and **AI-enhanced simulations** dominate operational phases. Transition management between phases is hindered by **fragmented, case-specific deployments** lacking standardized data schemas, necessitating **bidirectional communication protocols** and **unified engineering workflows** for seamless integration. Lifecycle governance approaches emphasize **stakeholder alignment**, **cost modeling**, and **operational efficiency**, but current implementations face challenges due to **high implementation costs** and **organizational barriers**, requiring a rethinking of system integration and collaboration across the asset lifecycle. The absence of **modular, scalable DT frameworks** tailored to AECO exacerbates these issues, as most frameworks (e.g., Eclipse Ditto, BaSyx) are designed for industrial/IoT applications and lack AECO-specific standards like BIM compliance."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The framework addresses **fidelity requirements** by bridging the gap between **static BIM representations** and the **dynamic nature of Digital Twins**, ensuring that enriched BIM models (with embedded control logic and sensor/actuator configurations) are transformed into **high-fidelity DT artifacts**. **Validation and verification** are implicitly supported through the **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric**, which evaluates the alignment between physical and digital systems. **Uncertainty sources** are mitigated via **real-time data exchange**, ensuring continuous bidirectional updates that adapt to operational changes. **Error detection** is facilitated by the **modular architecture**, where standardized extension points allow for the integration of **custom validation services** (e.g., anomaly detection, consistency checks). **Quality assurance** is embedded in the **MDE pipeline**, where automated transformations enforce semantic consistency between BIM and DT models, reducing manual intervention and associated errors. The framework’s **runtime synchronization** further ensures that deviations in physical conditions are reflected in the digital twin, enabling adaptive error mitigation."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT Framework employs a centralized hub architecture to facilitate bidirectional data exchange, integrating technologies like Eclipse Hono and Ditto for real-time communication. The network infrastructure prioritizes interoperability across heterogeneous systems, ensuring seamless connectivity between physical assets, BIM models, and operational analytics. While the text does not specify exact communication protocols (e.g., MQTT, OPC UA), the system’s modular design supports standardized extension points for custom services. Network topology and bandwidth/latency requirements are not detailed, but scalability from individual components to entire infrastructure systems is a key non-functional requirement. Redundancy mechanisms and security protocols (e.g., TLS, RBAC) are not mentioned, though the framework’s emphasis on maintainability and accessibility suggests robust underlying safeguards."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system prioritizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** as a critical non-functional requirement (NF_X), ensuring seamless integration with external systems through **standardized extension points** and **runtime interfaces**. While the document does not specify exact protocols (e.g., IFC, OPC UA, MQTT), it highlights the need for **data exchange protocols** and **interoperability standards** to connect with enterprise systems, other Digital Twins, or third-party services. The framework’s **modular architecture** supports **custom service integration**, implying compatibility with external tools and platforms. Dependencies on external systems are managed via the central hub, which acts as a **mediator for bidirectional data flow**. The system’s design aligns with the AECO domain’s need for **enterprise-wide integration**, though specific technologies (e.g., Eclipse Hono, Ditto) or detailed interface specifications are not mentioned in the provided snippets."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "Standardization in Digital Twin implementations for AECO is critically limited, with most frameworks (e.g., Eclipse Ditto, BaSyx) designed for **industrial/IoT applications** and lacking compliance with **AECO-specific standards like BIM**. Relevant industry standards are not explicitly listed, but challenges include **fragmented data schemas**, **proprietary ecosystems** (e.g., Bentley’s iTwin.js), and **manual integration efforts** due to absent automation. Interoperability standards are partially addressed through **connectivity and compliance frameworks** in frameworks like Eclipse Ditto and BaSyx, but these require **significant manual configuration** and do not natively support AECO workflows. Certification requirements and compliance frameworks are not detailed, though the document highlights the need for **unified engineering workflows** and **governance** to bridge gaps in standardization."
]
